title: munnozia senecionidis
intro: Munnozia senecionidis, also known as the Tree Groundsel, is a perennial plant species native to South America. It is used in traditional medicine for its hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, protecting the liver, and aiding in the treatment of digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Munnozia senecionidis are commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
